quick_skim.title: Seed-Guided Semi-Supervised Clustering by A-Contrario Anomaly Detection
quick_skim.abstract: This paper introduces a semi-supervised clustering framework grounded in the statistical duality between grouping principles and anomaly detection. We address the challenge of robust cluster definition in noisy environments -- a task where partitioning algorithms often over-assign outliers and density-based methods remain sensitive to heuristic global parameters. Drawing on \textit{a-contrario} statistical reasoning and Gestalt proximity principles, we define a cluster as a maximal subset of data points containing no anomalies relative to a null hypothesis of uniform randomness. Central to this approach is the Perception algorithm, which utilises a principled expectation-based threshold ($\mathbb{E} < 1$) to identify outliers without manual parameter tuning. By treating clustering as the dual of anomaly detection, we employ an iterative ``clustering-by-exclusion'' mechanism. The algorithm is seed-guided, leveraging minimal user-provided labels to initialise robust cluster medians and form initial groups, which are subsequently expanded by admitting non-anomalous points. This approach naturally isolates fringe points, isolated noise, and emerging unknown clusters. We evaluate the method on synthetic and real-world benchmarks, including image and text datasets represented through raw, linear-reduced, and neighbourhood-preserving embeddings. Results demonstrate that with as few as 10--30 seeds per cluster, the proposed method achieves competitive and often very strong performance under a practical low-tuning benchmarking protocol, while maintaining linear scalability with respect to both observations and dimensionality for a fixed number of seeded clusters and iterations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Semi-supervised clustering with seed guidance that robustly discovers groups, applicable to novel category discovery with known class seeds.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于种子引导的半监督聚类，可鲁棒地发现群组，适用于已知类种子下的新类别发现。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于种子引导的半监督聚类，可鲁棒地发现群组，适用于已知类种子下的新类别发现。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于种子引导的半监督聚类，可鲁棒地发现群组，适用于已知类种子下的新类别发现。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a seed-guided semi-supervised clustering method using a-contrario anomaly detection to define clusters as maximal anomaly-free subsets, robustly handling outliers; it is methodologically useful for novel class discovery in open-world scenarios when some known class seeds are available.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对噪声环境中传统聚类算法易误分异常点的问题，本文提出一种基于种子引导和反事实异常检测的半监督聚类框架，将簇定义为相对于随机零假设无异常的最大子集，并通过期望阈值自动识别离群点。实验表明，该方法无需人工参数即可鲁棒聚类，为开放世界新类发现任务提供了一种统计上可靠的聚类工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对噪声环境中传统聚类算法易误分异常点的问题，本文提出一种基于种子引导和反事实异常检测的半监督聚类框架，将簇定义为相对于随机零假设无异常的最大子集，并通过期望阈值自动识别离群点。实验表明，该方法无需人工参数即可鲁棒聚类，为开放世界新类发现任务提供了一种统计上可靠的聚类工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: generalized category discovery for unlabeled data with unknown classes
